name: Salazar v. Buono
question:
1) Can Mr. Buono's suit be maintained when he is merely offended by the fact that public land on which a cross is displayed is not a forum for other religious symbols?

2) Did the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit err in not giving effect to Congress's land swap where Sunrise Rock was made private land?
description: A case in which the Court held that an individual has standing to maintain a suit when he acquires a "judicially cognizable" interest in ensuring compliance with a previous judgment in his favor. The Court also held that a court may not order an injunction in a case when it fails to consider all the circumstances bearing on the need for preventive relief.
